page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 KB of L1 data cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier across multiple workloads. Single-core stress-ng CPU performance measures at 1841.19, while multi-core performance reaches 7764.36. Memory bandwidth tests show 426.28 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 59.12 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 39.31 GB/sec for large-block writes. In application-specific tests, the instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1615.0 and a multi-core score of 6005.0, with software compilation scoring 8218.0. Database operations perform at an average level, with Redis SET operations handling 7.99 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring 4039.27.

The hardware profile reveals distinct performance tradeoffs, particularly in machine learning tasks. While the instance handles small-scale language model inference efficiently, processing 753.54 tokens per second for a 135M parameter model, its performance drops significantly on larger models, yielding a weak 7.66 tokens per second for text generation on a 7B model. Given the lack of local storage and GPU acceleration, this instance is best suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database workloads rather than heavy data science or large-scale LLM deployment.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark results place its CPU and memory performance in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6005.0 and Redis SET operations reaching 7.99 million operations per second. While highly capable for standard workloads like static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations, it exhibits performance limitations on larger machine learning models.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for balanced enterprise workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering dedicated Intel Xeon processing and proportional memory for standard enterprise workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average-tier performance in database operations and software compilation
Suited for static web serving, application hosting, and moderate databases
